response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled polyglot software craftsman with genuine strengths in distributed systems, TDD, and developer tooling, but he falls critically short of the core minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ programming experience; the resume shows fewer than 12 months of C++ work (a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project), and C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. His career trajectory is firmly in web and full-stack development rather than cloud compute infrastructure or systems programming, and there is no evidence of sustained technical leadership over infrastructure or cloud platform teams. These gaps are too fundamental to overlook for a mid-senior technical lead position on an internal cloud compute platform.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
Consistent application of engineering best practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code craftsmanship) across multiple roles and teams
Broad infrastructure and DevOps familiarity including Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, CI servers, and artifact management
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (<1 year) versus the mandatory 8-year minimum, and the language is absent from his own skills summary
Career is predominantly web and full-stack (Node.js, TypeScript, Ember.js); no sustained work in cloud compute platforms, storage systems, or hardware-adjacent infrastructure
No formal technical leadership role over cloud or infrastructure engineers; mentoring is limited to community workshops and dojos rather than team-level engineering leadership

key_factors.factor: Engineering best practices and distributed systems exposure
